title: betula lenta var. populifolia
intro: Betula lenta var. populifolia, also known as yellow birch, is a deciduous tree that is a variety of the sweet birch tree (Betula lenta), characterized by its yellowish bark. The medicinal properties of yellow birch include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its salicylic acid content. The health benefits of yellow birch include its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Yellow birch can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
